package server

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/binary"
	"fmt"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/ollama/ollama/api"
	"github.com/ollama/ollama/manifest"
)

// TestSafetensorsHeaderMaxInt64 verifies that a crafted safetensors file with
// MaxInt64 as the header length causes a panic in parseSafetensors via
// make([]byte, 0, n).
func TestSafetensorsHeaderMaxInt64(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("OLLAMA_MODELS", t.TempDir())

	makeTemp := func(content string) string {
		l, err := manifest.NewLayer(strings.NewReader(content), "application/octet-stream")
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("NewLayer: %v", err)
		}
		return l.Digest
	}

	// Craft a safetensors file with MaxInt64 as the header length.
	var buf bytes.Buffer
	var n int64 = 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF
	binary.Write(&buf, binary.LittleEndian, n)

	model := makeTemp(buf.String())
	config := makeTemp(`{"architectures": ["LlamaForCausalLM"], "vocab_size": 32000}`)
	tokenizer := makeTemp(`{"version": "1.0"}`)

	files := map[string]string{
		"model.safetensors": model,
		"config.json":       config,
		"tokenizer.json":    tokenizer,
	}

	defer func() {
		if r := recover(); r != nil {
			t.Logf("PANIC: %v", r)
		}
	}()

	_, err := convertFromSafetensors(files, nil, false, func(resp api.ProgressResponse) {})
	fmt.Printf("convertFromSafetensors returned err=%v\n", err)
}
